test.name: A baptism naming a parent for an existing child is linked via pe_ entries only — the ParentChild edge is deferred to the pipeline
test.description: Guards the SKILL.md §7 boundary clarification for the gray zone the jens-nielsen e2e f3 finding exposed: a single, NON-household record that merely STATES a parent-child link for a person already in the tree. This is distinct from a co-resident household skeleton (census), where person-evidence DOES write the edges at link time (ut_person_evidence_021) — and it routes the same as a marriage relationship assertion (ut_person_evidence_022): pe_ links only, relationship deferred. The flynn-baptism-names-mother tree holds Patrick (I1) and his father Thomas (I2, via RT1) but NOT the mother. Baptism assertion a_001 (source S2 / src_001, original+primary+direct) names Bridget Doyle as Patrick's mother; a_002 is her name assertion. The correct person-evidence behavior is: create Bridget as a stub person, create a pe_ link for BOTH parties (Patrick I1 and the new Bridget stub) — WITHOUT creating the ParentChild relationship edge or calling tree_edit add_relationship for it. The concluded parentage edge is written later by proof-conclusion → tree-edit. Per TREE_OWNERSHIP_TABLE, research-schema-spec §8 (link-time materialization carries facts + names, not relationship edges), and SKILL.md §7.

input.user_message: person-evidence: assertion a_001 is Patrick Flynn's (I1) Kilrush baptism, which names his mother as Bridget Doyle (source src_001 / tree source S2). Bridget Doyle is not yet in the tree. Link this baptism's parentage assertion to the right persons.

judge_context: The core behavior under test is LANE DISCIPLINE for a single non-household record that STATES a parentage: person-evidence resolves identity and records pe_ links; it does NOT create the tree ParentChild relationship edge. Grade Correctness primarily on that.

Bridget Doyle matches no existing person (the tree holds only Patrick I1 and his father Thomas I2), so person-evidence must CREATE A NEW STUB for her — a synthetic id such as I3, gender Female, a name with surname Doyle (as the baptism gives). Creating a stub person is within person-evidence's ownership (the `persons` section).
person-evidence must create person_evidence (pe_) entries linking a_001 to BOTH parties (Patrick I1 and the new Bridget stub), since a relationship assertion bears on both persons it names.

It is WRONG for person-evidence to create the ParentChild relationship edge between Bridget and Patrick, and WRONG to call tree_edit add_relationship for it. That belongs to proof-conclusion → tree-edit, which own the `relationships` section. A run that adds the ParentChild edge should FAIL Correctness — the skill should explicitly DEFER the relationship to the downstream pipeline. This is the census-household EXCEPTION's mirror image: a baptism is not a co-resident household skeleton, so the edge is deferred, not written.

a_001's record_persona_id is null, so same_person cannot run and the new person_evidence entries' match_score must be null — the links rest on correlation (the register names Thomas Flynn and Bridget Doyle as Patrick's parents).
The new Bridget stub rests on the single baptism with no independent corroboration yet, so her person_evidence link is probable at most — confident would over-claim.
The stub should be minimal (gender + a name); person-evidence should NOT populate Bridget's birth/other facts — proof-conclusion populates stub facts later.

COMPLETENESS GRADING NOTE for the judge: score Completeness on whether person-evidence created the Bridget stub and both pe_ links and correctly deferred the ParentChild edge — not on any downstream step (proof-conclusion, tree-edit, check-warnings) that is out of this skill's lane and unavailable in the unit harness.
